from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Group triples by subject to allow multiple entities
    from collections import defaultdict
    subject_map = defaultdict(list)
    for t in triples:
        subject_map[t.subject].append(t)

    # Simple predicate → phrase templates
    # Helper to clean numeric objects (e.g., remove trailing .0)
    def _clean_obj(o: str) -> str:
        o = o.strip('"')
        # remove commas inside numbers for readability
        if o.replace(".", "", 1).isdigit():
            if o.endswith(".0"):
                o = o[:-2]
        # Simplify language objects like "English language" to "English"
        if o.lower().endswith(" language"):
            o = o[: -len(" language")]
        return o

    # More natural templates; some include the subject directly
    # Phrase templates (subject omitted) for smoother combination into a single sentence
    phrase_templates = {
        "cityServed": "serves the city of {obj}",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "is situated {obj} metres above sea level",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": "is situated {obj} feet above sea level",
        "runwayLength": "has runway length of {obj} metres",
        "runwayName": "has the runway name of {obj}",
        "location": "is located in {obj}",
        "operatingOrganisation": "is operated by {obj}",
        "country": "is in {obj}",
        "isPartOf": "is part of {obj}",
        "icaoLocationIdentifier": "has ICAO code {obj}",
        "locationIdentifier": "has location identifier {obj}",
        "iataLocationIdentifier": "has IATA code {obj}",
        "1stRunwayLengthFeet": "has a first runway length of {obj} feet",
        "1stRunwayLengthMetre": "has a first runway length of {obj} metres",
        "1stRunwaySurfaceType": "has first runway surface {obj}",
        "1stRunwayNumber": "has first runway number {obj}",
        "runwaySurfaceType": "has runway surface {obj}",
        "leader": "leader is {obj}",
        "capital": "capital is {obj}",
        "language": "language is {obj}",
        "officialLanguage": "official language is {obj}",
        "currency": "uses currency {obj}",
        "owner": "is owned by {obj}",
        "foundingYear": "was founded in {obj}",
        "founder": "was founded by {obj}",
        "postalCode": "has postal code {obj}",
        "areaCode": "has area code {obj}",
        "largestCity": "has largest city {obj}",
        "countySeat": "has county seat {obj}",
        "mayor": "has mayor {obj}",
        "leaderParty": "has leader party {obj}",
        "leaderTitle": "has leader title {obj}",
        "chief": "has chief {obj}",
        "hubAirport": "has hub airport {obj}",
        "regionServed": "serves region {obj}",
        "nativeName": "has native name {obj}",
        "class": "class is {obj}",
        "division": "division is {obj}",
        "order": "order is {obj}",
        "demonym": "demonym is {obj}",
    }

    # First, try to create combined sentences for common patterns (e.g., cityServed + mayor)
    linked_sentences = []
    used_triples = set()

    for airport, tlist in subject_map.items():
        city = None
        # find cityServed triple
        for t in tlist:
            if t.predicate == "cityServed":
                city = _clean_obj(t.object)
                used_triples.add(t)
                break
        if city and city in subject_map:
            mayor_obj = None
            for t2 in subject_map[city]:
                if t2.predicate == "mayor":
                    mayor_obj = _clean_obj(t2.object)
                    used_triples.add(t2)
                    break
            if mayor_obj:
                linked_sentences.append(f"{mayor_obj} is the mayor of {city}, which is served by {airport}.")

    sentences = []
    for subject, tlist in subject_map.items():
        parts = []
        special_sentences = []
        for t in tlist:
            if t in used_triples:
                continue
            obj = _clean_obj(t.object)

            # --- special full‑sentence predicates --------------------------------
            if t.predicate == "capital":
                special_sentences.append(f"The capital of {subject} is {obj}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "demonym":
                special_sentences.append(f"{subject} demonym is {obj}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "leader":
                special_sentences.append(f"The leader of {subject} is {obj}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "language":
                special_sentences.append(f"{obj} is spoken in {subject}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "leaderParty":
                special_sentences.append(f"The leader party of {subject} is {obj}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "runwayLength":
                special_sentences.append(f"The runway length of {subject} is {obj}.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "1stRunwayLengthFeet":
                special_sentences.append(f"The first runway length of {subject} is {obj} feet.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "1stRunwayLengthMetre":
                special_sentences.append(f"The first runway length of {subject} is {obj} metres.")
                continue
            if t.predicate == "runwayName":
                special_sentences.append(f"The runway of {subject} is named {obj}.")
                continue

            # --- runway length predicates with ordinal handling -------
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
                prefix = t.predicate.replace("RunwayLengthFeet", "")
                sentence = f"The {prefix.lower()} runway length of {subject} is {obj} feet."
                special_sentences.append(sentence)
                continue
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):
                prefix = t.predicate.replace("RunwayLengthMetre", "")
                sentence = f"The {prefix.lower()} runway length of {subject} is {obj} metres."
                special_sentences.append(sentence)
                continue
            # --- runway surface predicates with linked entity information -------
            if t.predicate.endswith("RunwaySurfaceType"):
                prefix = t.predicate.replace("RunwaySurfaceType", "")
                sentence = f"The {prefix.lower()} runway at {subject} is made from {obj}"
                related_triples = subject_map.get(obj, [])
                descriptors = []
                for rel in related_triples:
                    rel_obj = _clean_obj(rel.object)
                    if rel.predicate == "class":
                        descriptors.append(f"class {rel_obj}")
                    elif rel.predicate == "division":
                        descriptors.append(f"division {rel_obj}")
                    elif rel.predicate == "order":
                        descriptors.append(f"order {rel_obj}")
                if descriptors:
                    sentence += " which is " + " and ".join(descriptors)
                sentence += "."
                special_sentences.append(sentence)
                continue

            tmpl = phrase_templates.get(t.predicate)
            if tmpl:
                parts.append(tmpl.format(obj=obj))
            else:
                pred = t.predicate.replace('_', ' ')
                parts.append(f"{pred} {obj}")
        if parts:
            if len(parts) == 1:
                sentence = f"{subject} {parts[0]}."
            else:
                sentence = f"{subject} " + ", ".join(parts[:-1]) + ", and " + parts[-1] + "."
            sentences.append(sentence)
        sentences.extend(special_sentences)

    # prepend linked sentences (they already form a complete sentence)
    all_sentences = linked_sentences + sentences
    return " ".join(all_sentences).strip()

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
